Chief Complaint and History of Present Illness
Ronald is a 69y old Male who presents with a primary complaint of B knee pain, longstanding, has
failed HA and steroid injections as recently as December 2025, using meioxicam
Patient does not consume tobacco. 1-2 daily- beers
Patient does not use recreational drugs.
Constitutional: Reports Good general health lately.
Skin: No Rash. No Itching. No Dry Skin. No Pigmentation. No change in hair or nalls. No varicose
Reports Trouble breathing. Reports Seasonal Allergies.

Vision Change. No Double Vision. No Eye Pain. No Eye Disease or Injury. Does not wear glasses or

Constitutional: The patient appears to be stated age, cooperative and In no distress. The patient
is able to answer questions and carry on a conversation without use of accessory muscles of
respiration. The patient Is well nourlshed and hydrated with moist mucous membranes.
Eye: Externally: Normal appearance of the conjunctiva, eyelids with normal ocular symmetry.
Sclera are normal without hemorrhage or Icterus. Pupils are equal round and reactive to light and
Ears, Nose, Mouth, Throat: Normal external appearance of the ears and nose. Normal bilateral
auditory acuity. Normal appearance of the oropharynx without pallor or cyanosis and with moist
mucosa. Normal appearance of the teeth and gums without and dental carles. Normal appearance
of the palate with normal appearance rise of the uvula.
Neck: No jugular venous elevation. Normal thyroid exam without enlargement, tenderness or
nodules. Normal parotid exam without enlargement, tenderness or nodules. No palpable masses
or nodules. No palpable No palpable lymphadenopathy In the anterior cervical, posterior cervical,
Respiratory: Normal respiratory effort without use of accessory muscles of respiration or pursed
lip breathing. Normal external appearance of the chest without barrel chest, pectus, kyphosis or
scoliosis. Normal breath sounds without wheezing, rhonchi or rales. Normal percussive exam
without dullness or evidence of consolldation.
Lymphatics: Lymph Nodes Normal. No evidence of cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal
Musculoskeletal: B knee MJL pain, no effusion, good AROM, apparent MCL laxity W stress
Extremities: Normal appearance of the upper and lower extremities without obvious deformity.
There Is no digital clubbing, cyanosis or edema.
Skin: Externally there is no evidence of xanthomas or cellulitis. This is no dermatitis or rash. There are no
ulcers or wounds present. There is no evidence of venous stasis changes. The capillary refill is < 5 seconds.
Neurological/Psychlatrio: Externally the face Is symmetrical. There is normal sharp and dull
sensation In the hands and feet bilaterally. The cranial nerves are Intact II - XII. The patient moves
the upper and lower extremities equally. The finger to nose exam is normal. Appropriate mood and
affect. Alert and oriented to person, time and place. Patient appears to have intact judgement and
Insight into the current health issues.
